8.reason: [The loop prints exactly 10 characters regardless of N, which is incorrect. It should print only 'p' numbers (i.e., N numbers), not 10. This causes wrong output format and extra garbage characters when N < 10.]

16.reason: [This fails for i = 10 because '0' + 10 is not the character "10" but a single non-digit character (ASCII 58). The program cannot handle N=10 correctly since it tries to store two-digit number '10' as a single char.]
